methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $400M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
ClearSpace, a Series A Space & Aerospace company founded by Luc Piguet, is currently valued at $400M post-money and has raised $36M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, with 3 directly comparable private companies used for quantitative anchoring.

Two valuation methods are applicable here. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage step-up, implying $800M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, adjacent-stage comparables—Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B Series B), Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), and Hubble Network ($400M Series B)—yielding a median of $1.3B and a range of $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because ClearSpace's revenue is not disclosed, a common constraint in early-stage private company valuation.

Triangulating the two applicable methods yields a valuation range of $680M to $1.5B, with a median of $1.1B. This suggests the current $400M valuation sits below the lower bound, reflecting typical Series A discounting. Key uncertainties include technology development risk, customer acquisition trajectory, and the significant dispersion within the peer set ($400M to $2.0B), which reflects the heterogeneity of space-sector business models and commercial maturity.
